title: autonoe haemorrhoidalis
intro: Autonoe haemorrhoidalis, also known as Witch Hazel, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high content of tannins and gallic acid. The medicinal properties of Autonoe haemorrhoidalis make it beneficial for treating various health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Witch Hazel, such as creams, ointments, and infusions, can be used to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.
